PAI AeroNews

Drones Mar 19, 2026

FCC Exempts Four Drones From Import Ban

March action follows January temporary exemptions tied to broader foreign UAS restrictions.

The FCC's selective drone import exemptions signal regulatory flexibility in managing foreign UAS restrictions while allowing critical operations to continue, creating opportunities for operators to navigate compliance requirements through formal exemption processes.

French A400M makes first-ever landing on Arctic sea ice in Greenland

A French Air and Space Force A400M Atlas has landed on Arctic sea ice in northern Greenland, in what France’s… The post French A400M makes first-ever landing on Arctic sea ice in Greenland appeared first on AeroTime.

The A400M's successful Arctic sea ice landing demonstrates critical operational capability for military transport aircraft in extreme environments, expanding potential deployment scenarios for allied forces in strategic polar regions.

Drones Mar 19, 2026

Robinson Helicopter Details New Unmanned Business Unit, Cargo UAS Project With Sikorsky

Robinson Helicopter on March 9 announced the launch of its new Robinson Unmanned business unit, and detailed a new unmanned cargo helicopter offering utilizing Sikorsky’s autonomy capability. With Robinson Unmanned, […] The post Robinson Helicopter Details New Unmanned Business Unit, Cargo UAS Pr...

Robinson Helicopter's new unmanned business unit and partnership with Sikorsky on autonomous cargo helicopters represents a strategic pivot toward high-growth UAS markets, positioning the manufacturer to capture emerging opportunities in unmanned logistics and autonomous aviation systems.

Military Mar 19, 2026

A-10s Now Hunting Iranian Fast Attack Craft in the Strait of Hormuz

U.S. Air Force A-10s in maritime attack role are hunting small Iranian fast attack boats that could threaten shipping or naval forces in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. Air Force A-10 Thundebolt II jets supporting operation Epic Fury have started flying a different type of mission. As explained by...

The U.S. Air Force is adapting the A-10 Thunderbolt II for maritime attack missions against fast attack craft in the Strait of Hormuz, demonstrating the platform's versatility beyond traditional close air support roles.

See The Aviationist →

Safety Mar 19, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to systematically select and implement controls while documenting their acceptance of residual risks that cannot be eliminated, moving regulatory compliance from theoretical analysis to operational accountability.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA to Cover Progress 94 Spacecraft Launch, Space Station Docking

NASA will provide live coverage of the launch and docking of a Roscosmos cargo spacecraft carrying about three tons of food, fuel, and supplies for the crew aboard the International Space Station. The unpiloted Roscosmos Progress 94 resupply spacecraft is scheduled to launch at 7:59 a.m. EDT (4:5...

The continued ISS resupply missions demonstrate ongoing international space cooperation that maintains critical infrastructure for long-duration spaceflight operations, relevant to commercial space ventures pursuing similar orbital logistics capabilities.

See NASA →

Airbus Built The A350F, But Why Hasn’t Boeing Produced A 787 Freighter?

Boeing is addressing the A350F with another aircraft.

Boeing's delay in launching a 787 freighter variant while Airbus moves forward with the A350F represents a strategic market gap that could affect long-haul cargo competitiveness and supply chain economics for both manufacturers.

Airbus's pursuit of damages against Pratt & Whitney for delivery delays signals escalating supply chain tensions that could reshape OEM-supplier relationships and impact aircraft production schedules across the industry.

With this green light from the FAA, Boeing is one step closer to its goal of certifying its long-awaited 777-9 aircraft.

Boeing's 777-9 certification progress through FAA milestones signals the imminent entry into service for the manufacturer's most advanced widebody aircraft, potentially reshaping long-haul fleet economics for carriers.

AeroVironment's $200 million acquisition of ESAero strategically expands its manufacturing capacity and electric/hybrid UAS capabilities, positioning the company to scale production and product development in the competitive unmanned systems market.

General Aviation Mar 19, 2026

FAA Aviation Safety at Oshkosh

Go behind the scenes at EAA AirVenture with the FAA's Aviation Safety organization. Meet the people who help ensure safety at this event and provide essential information resources to the general aviation community.

The FAA's presence at EAA AirVenture demonstrates the agency's commitment to proactive safety engagement with general aviation pilots through direct community outreach and resource sharing at the industry's largest fly-in event.

Commercial Mar 19, 2026

How Long Did American Airlines Operate The Boeing 747 For?

Find out how long American Airlines flew the Boeing 747 and what led to the early retirement of the iconic “Queen of the Skies.”

American Airlines' early retirement of the Boeing 747 reflects the commercial aviation industry's shift toward more fuel-efficient twin-engine aircraft and changing long-haul route economics in the 21st century.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

Meet the Platypi: NASA’s Newest Astronaut Candidate Class

Ten explorers are currently training at NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston to become flight-eligible astronauts. Selected in 2025, the astronaut candidates are building the technical and operational skills needed for future missions to the International Space Station, the Moon, and eventually...

NASA's 2025 astronaut candidate class represents the pipeline of human spaceflight expertise that will support commercial spaceflight partnerships and sustained lunar operations, creating demand for specialized aerospace training and certification infrastructure.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA’s Roman Observatory Passes Final Major Prelaunch Tests

NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope team recently blasted the observatory with extreme sound, shook it, and listened to its electronic hum. Roman passed all three assessments, which aimed to confirm that the observatory will withstand launch conditions and function as expected in space. The ...

Space telescope advancement demonstrates engineering validation methodologies applicable to aerospace systems requiring extreme environmental resilience during launch and orbital deployment.

See NASA →

Industry Mar 19, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through advanced technologies offers a dual pathway to accommodate growing air traffic while addressing environmental concerns, making efficiency improvements critical for aviation's long-term sustainability.

See MDPI →

Safety Mar 19, 2026

How One Of The World's Most Notorious Fraudsters Posed As A Pilot To Fly For Free

Made extra-famous through the hollywood film ‘Catch Me If You Can’, legendary conman turned security consultant Frank Abagnale managed to fly for free aboard some 250 flights by posing as a deadheading pilot - a flight crew member being repositioned to another city for operational purposes. In a ...

Frank Abagnale's historic exploitation of pilot credential vulnerabilities demonstrates critical gaps in airline crew verification protocols that modern aviation security must continually address to prevent unauthorized access to aircraft.

See Simple Flying →

Russian Su-30 briefly violates Estonian airspace near Vaindloo, NATO jets respond

A Russian Su-30 fighter jet briefly entered Estonian airspace near Vaindloo Island on March 18, 2026, prompting a… The post Russian Su-30 briefly violates Estonian airspace near Vaindloo, NATO jets respond appeared first on AeroTime.

Escalating airspace violations by Russian military aircraft over NATO member states demonstrate the continued need for robust air defense capabilities and international coordination to maintain Eastern European airspace integrity.

DJI Mini 4K now priced like a budget accessory

The DJI Mini 4K, one of the easiest, most beginner-friendly drones you can buy right now, has just dropped to its lowest price ever. And we’re not talking about a small discount deal. These are the kind of prices that make you double-check if it’s real. more…

The DJI Mini 4K's aggressive pricing strategy at record lows signals intensifying competition in the consumer drone market, which may influence how aviation professionals evaluate commercial drone platform economics and market accessibility.

NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings

NASA is proposing a sharp increase in the rate of robotic lunar lander missions, a move that has excited but also puzzled the space community. The post NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings appeared first on SpaceNews.

NASA's acceleration of robotic lunar landing missions will drive significant demand for specialized aerospace manufacturing, testing infrastructure, and launch services across the commercial space sector.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI

Finnish satellite manufacturer ReOrbit has signed a contract with asset-financing company SLI for two small geostationary orbit communications satellites. The post ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI appeared first on SpaceNews.

The emergence of specialized small satellite manufacturers serving niche GEO markets demonstrates how innovation in space infrastructure can create new revenue streams and operational efficiencies for aviation and telecommunications industries.

Everdrone raises SEK 36 million – enters commercial phase for drone services in emergency healthcare

Everdrone currently operates the world’s first operational drone service integrated into the emergency response chain

Everdrone's successful funding round and integration into emergency healthcare response demonstrates the commercial viability of drone services in critical infrastructure, signaling a significant expansion opportunity for aviation operators in specialized logistics markets.

uAvionix Introduces New ping978EC ADS-B Transceiver Enabling UK BVLOS UAS Operators to Meet Latest CAA Electronic Conspicuity Regulations

uAvionix, a leader in aviation communications, navigation, and surveillance data solutions, is excited to announce

uAvionix's new ping978EC transceiver addresses the CAA's electronic conspicuity mandate for UK BVLOS drone operations, streamlining regulatory compliance for commercial UAS operators.

DJI's aggressive pricing on the Avata 2 signals imminent product transitions in the consumer FPV market, requiring commercial operators and integrators to evaluate timing for equipment upgrades and fleet modernization.

Here’s How Far The Airbus A380 Can Fly Without Refueling

Discover the A380's remarkable 8,000 nautical mile range and how revolutionary technology allowed this giant to fly so far.

The A380's 8,000 nautical mile range demonstrates how advanced aerodynamic design and fuel efficiency enable ultra-long-range operations for the world's largest passenger aircraft, supporting point-to-point connectivity on transcontinental routes.

Drones Mar 19, 2026

DJI Mini 3 drone just entered impulse-buy territory

For years, there’s been a clear line in the drone world: spend real money, or settle for something that feels like a toy. The DJI Mini 3 just erased that line. more…

The DJI Mini 3's price-to-performance ratio represents a significant market shift that could accelerate drone adoption across commercial and recreational segments, challenging traditional barriers to entry in the unmanned aircraft industry.
